zh.lifeSummary: Sir Roger Seymour de St. Maur是Roger St. Maur I和Eve Marshall的兒子，於1270年出生在威爾士蒙姆爾福。他和Alice Lisle至少有1個兒子。他去世於1366年在英國威爾斯紐波特享年96歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir Roger Seymour de St. Maur是Roger St. Maur I和Eve Marshall的儿子，于1270年出生在威尔士蒙姆爾福。他和Alice Lisle至少有1个儿子。他去世于1366年在英国威尔斯紐波特享年96岁。

nameMeanings.name: Seymour
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (London): of Norman origin, a habitational name from Saint-Maur-des-Fossés (Seine), and possibly also from Saint-Maur-des-Bois (Manche) and Saint-Maur-sur-Loire (Touraine), named from the dedication of their churches to Saint Maur (see   Moore   3). The family of this name who have held the title Duke of Somerset since 1547 are said by some to have originated in Saint-Maur-des-Fossés (Seine), while others claim they are from Saint-Maur-sur-Loire (Touraine). They rose to prominence under the Tudors, when Edward Seymour (died 1552) held a high position of office under Henry VIII, who was married to Edward's sister, Jane. The Norman name has been established in Ireland since at least the 17th century.

variants.name: Moore
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Seymore
meanings: English: variant of   Seymour  .

variants.name: Semar
meanings: Germanized form of French Saint Marc, the name of a Huguenot family that migrated to the Palatinate.
English: variant of   Seymour  . This surname is very rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: St.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized spelling of French   St  .   Denis  .

variants.name: St
meanings: Southern French: variant of   St  .   Romain  .

variants.name: Denis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Jacques, Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Serge, Yvan, Alphonse, Edouard, Francois, Gaetane.  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Roberto, Angel, Francisco, Joaquin, Juan, Manuel, Miguel, Ramon, Rolando.

French, Walloon, Breton, West Indian (mainly Haiti), German, Dutch, English, and Portuguese; Spanish and Galician (Denís): from the personal name Denis, Spanish and Galician Denís, from Latin Dionysius ‘(follower) of Dionysos’ (see Dennis ). Compare Dennee .
Ukrainian and Rusyn: variant transliteration of   Denys  , a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Maur
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: nickname for someone supposedly resembling a mouse, in appearance or timidity, from Middle High German mūs ‘mouse’.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from German Maus ‘mouse’, one of the most common of the unflattering surnames imposed on Jews by non-Jewish government officials in 18th- and 19th-century central Europe.

variants.name: Muus
meanings: German (Hamburg): nickname for someone supposedly resembling a mouse, in appearance or timidity, from Middle High German mūs ‘mouse’. Compare Maus and Muhs .

variants.name: Muske
meanings: North German (also Müske): nickname from a diminutive of Middle Low German mūs ‘mouse’ (see Maus 1).

variants.name: Mauss
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Wilhelm.
German: variant of   Maus  .

variants.name: Meisel
meanings: German: nickname for someone supposedly resembling a mouse, in appearance or timidity, from Middle High German mūs ‘mouse’.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from German Maus ‘mouse’, one of the most common of the unflattering surnames imposed on Jews by non-Jewish government officials in 18th- and 19th-century central Europe.

variants.name: Mousel
meanings: Americanized form of German Mäusl, from a diminutive of Maus .

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Roger Seymour de St. Maur was born in 1270, in Penhow, Monmouthshire, Wales as the son of Roger St. Maur I and Eve Marshall. He had at least 1 son with Alice Lisle. He died in 1366, in Newport, Wales, United Kingdom, at the age of 96.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Roger Seymour de St. Maur est né en 1270, à Penhow, Monmouthshire, Galles, fils de Roger St. Maur I et Eve Marshall. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Alice Lisle. Il est décédé en 1366, à Newport, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 96 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Sir Roger Seymour de St. Maur wurde 1270 in Penhow, Monmouthshire, Wales, als Sohn von Roger St. Maur I und Eve Marshall geboren. Er hatte mit Alice Lisle mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1366 im Alter von 96 Jahren in Newport, Wales, United Kingdom.

ht.Newport, Wales, United Kingdom: Newport, Pays de Galles, United Kingdom

it.lifeSummary: Sir Roger Seymour de St. Maur è nato il nel 1270 nel Penhow, Monmouthshire, Galles come figlio di Roger St. Maur I e Eve Marshall. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Alice Lisle. Morì nel 1366, nel Newport, Galles, Regno Unito, all'età di 96 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Roger Seymour de St. Maur(은)는 1270년 웨일스 몬모우스실르 펜호에서 Roger St. Maur I(와)과 Eve Marshall의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Alice Lisle(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1366년 영국 웨일스 뉴포트에서 96세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir Roger Seymour de St. Maur nasceu em 1270, em Penhow, Monmouthshire, Gales filho de Roger St. Maur I e Eve Marshall. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Alice Lisle. Ele faleceu em 1366, em Newport, Gales, United Kingdom, com 96 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Roger Seymour de St. Maur родился в 1270 г. в местности пеноу, монмоутшир, Уэльс. Его родителей звали Roger St. Maur I и Eve Marshall. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Alice Lisle. Он умер в 1366 г. в местности нюпорт, Уэльс, Великобритания в возрасте 96 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Sir Roger Seymour de St. Maur nació en 1270, en Penhow, Monmouthshire, Gales como hijo de Roger St. Maur I y Eve Marshall. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Alice Lisle. Murió en 1366, en Newport, Gales, Reino Unido, a la edad de 96 años.
